data.description:
Rec 6 of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md. The JSON Schema system (SSOT schemas, $ref-shared common.schema.json, coverage guard) is a strength — keep it. Three gaps to freeze NOW while breaking changes are cheap (announced internally 2026-06-09; no real consumers yet):
(a) Exit-code contract: 0/1/2/10 are load-bearing for agents but scattered through script headers. Author one canonical doc (docs/exit-codes.md or a section in output-schemas.md) + a conformance test.
(b) Error envelope: extend the error_envelope schema to every command's failure mode so agents never parse stderr prose. Error-shape changes are breaking — decide now.
(c) Event-schema versioning: docs/event-schema.md is the true wire format between clones of DIFFERENT rebar versions; a 500-person rollout means mixed versions sharing one origin/tickets soon. Declare it formally versioned, and add + test a reducer rule: unknown event TYPEs are preserved-and-ignored.
(d) Close the mcp_server.py pydantic-model-vs-schema equivalence pin fully (test_schema_outputs.py is partial).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Canonical exit-code contract documented + conformance-tested
- [ ] error_envelope covers every command failure mode; documented as the only machine-readable error channel
- [ ] Event schema carries an explicit version + tested unknown-TYPE forward-compat rule
- [ ] MCP outputSchemas pinned equivalent to the JSON Schemas in CI
data.title: Contract freeze (in-window): exit-code contract, error envelope everywhere, event-schema versioning
